entry.title: Washington Post: 21 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Italy’s Mario Draghi resigns; new elections are set for September
  • The world’s oldest known male panda, beloved in Hong Kong, dies at 35
  • European Central Bank takes big step to address rising inflation fears
  • In a first, India elects tribal woman to be president
  • Biden: U.S. military does not support Nancy Pelosi visiting Taiwan
  • How Russia is laying the groundwork for its annexation of Ukraine
  • Russia resumes gas flows to Germany after Nord Stream maintenance
  • Ukraine’s first lady says her son, once into arts, now wants to be a soldier
  • Russia and Syria conducted dozens of illegal ‘double tap’ strikes, report says
  • Ukraine live briefing: Russia restarts gas to Germany; CIA sees no sign Putin is ill
  • World chess champion to give up title because he’s just ‘not motivated’
  • China fines Didi $1.2 billion for breaking data-security laws
  • Thieves stole ‘Precious Blood’ relic. It reemerged at detective’s door.
  • Methanol found in bodies of 21 teens who died in South African nightclub
  • CIA chief says 15,000 Russians killed in war, dismisses Putin health rumors
  • Watching Japan reckon with a rare shooting, through an American prism
  • Myanmar’s junta can’t win the civil war it started
  • Lourdes Grobet, photographer of lucha libre’s masked stars, dies at 81
  • Ukraine live briefing: Russia expands territorial ambitions as U.S. weighs sending fighter jets to Kyiv
  • Why European homes (usually) don’t have air conditioning
  • EXPLAINER: Who gains or loses, what's next in Italy crisis
  • Court closes restraining order case against Ricky Martin
  • Israel aims to stop Russian move against Jewish nonprofit
  • Turkey denies carrying out deadly strikes in northern Iraq
  • Rio de Janeiro police conduct deadly raid in Alemao favelas
  • Officer fatally shot 15-foot snake that was strangling man, police say
  • Louisiana abortion clinics stay open after judge temporarily blocks ban
  • Trump White House aide goes on sexist tirade, calls Jan. 6 panel ‘anti-White’
  • Two men indicted in Texas tractor-trailer tragedy that left 53 dead
  • Video shows California police officer shoot man as he runs away
  • A U.K. meteorologist warned of deadly heat. He was told to cheer up.
  • Former Minneapolis officer sentenced to 30 months for violating Floyd’s civil rights
  • Lightning strike kills one soldier, injures nine at Georgia base
  • Steve Bannon trial live updates: Closing arguments set for Friday
  • Post Politics Now: ‘Folks, I’m doing great,’ Biden says after positive coronavirus test
  • Elaine Luria prepares to lead Jan. 6 hearing, connect Trump to violence
  • Even a day after Jan. 6, Trump balked at condemning the violence
  • Prosecution rests contempt case against Bannon after two witnesses
  • Rudy Giuliani ordered to testify before Georgia special grand jury
  • Prosecutors wrap up case against Bannon after two witnesses
  • Officials reorganize HHS to boost pandemic response
  • Amazon makes $3.9 billion bet on patient health care
  • Zipmex joins growing list of crypto exchanges to block withdrawals
  • Former Coinbase manager arrested on crypto insider-trading charges
  • Mortgage rates pause ahead of next week’s Federal Reserve meeting
  • A worker was nervous about going on disability. Readers share advice.
  • Activision Blizzard staff walk out, protesting loss of abortion rights
  • Facebook forsakes friends and family to compete with TikTok
  • D.C. family peels away older renovations and unveils dream home
  • Report: Homes deemed unaffordable in most U.S. counties
  • AT&T Jumps Out of the Media Pan and Into the Dividend Fire
  • Biden’s Covid Diagnosis Is a Wake-Up Call for America
  • ECB’s Crisis Plan Fails to Convince Bond Traders
  • Amazon’s Private-Label Business Is a Losing Proposition
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • How Sri Lanka Landed in a Crisis and What It Means: QuickTake
  • Understanding the Unraveling of Tunisia’s Revolution
  • How Thailand Is Blazing Trails on Gay Rights, Legalizing Marijuana
  • China’s ‘zero covid’ policy has been a nightmare for U.S. diplomats
  • Biden’s covid diagnosis is a teaching moment for the country
  • Electoral Count Act reform won’t save our republic. But it could help preserve it.
  • Your questions about covid-19, answered by Dr. Leana Wen
  • What do we owe kids for all they sacrificed during covid?
  • Republicans could change government the way they changed the courts
  • Tech giants want to squelch a bill that could give consumers more choice
  • Won’t address climate change for yourselves? How about for the guy who designs heat maps?
  • Why progressive Democrats are losing ground
  • Italy’s latest government crisis is no laughing matter
  • Climate change is a crisis. Invoking emergency powers won’t solve it.
  • Do the justices regret blowing up the Supreme Court’s reputation yet?
  • Biden’s diagnosis is a reminder: We must still make things better
  • Tensions between the U.S. and Mexico could affect the fate of a drug lord
  • If our freedom to travel is impinged, what’s next?
  • What being angry with your party means
  • From the World Series to AAA
  • Arlington’s housing proposal is missing something
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 21 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 21 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-21 19:30PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • What Couples Therapy Australia can teach us about our relationships
  • The great COVID-19 gap: Why is pop culture ignoring the pandemic?
  • Stop, operate and listen: my minor surgery was a minor attraction
  • ‘A reasonable response’: Why young Rooster felt guilty over ACL heartache
  • ASX eyes flat start despite Wall Street rise
  • ‘Her vision was blurry’: the mother-daughter duo who conquered Everest
  • Of all the gin pricepoints in the world ... Why’s craft gin so expensive?
  • Public schools face funding shortfall as parent contributions drop
  • This stunning garden is built from recycling that didn’t need to be thrown away
  • Rail loop rethink is first major test for new planning minister
  • Perth mayor wants ideas on bringing residential boom to the city
  • Recipient of COVID fine tells of confusion as he challenges ticket in court
  • Smoke exposure drives jump in Perth ED presentations during bushfires
  • The secrets to growing a fern both indoors and out
  • Surgery delays ramp up amid rise in post-viral complications
  • Swift action will reduce the environmental and economic impact of climate change
  • Why reforming Victorian Labor won’t be part of Andrews legacy
  • Blame Askin and the ABC for the Opera House’s old acoustic flaws, not our unsung hero Peter Hall
  • Pothole plague: rain-damaged roads trigger thousands of calls for help
  • When her students’ vocabulary standards started to slip, this teacher took parents into class
  • Unions want the right to work from home included in enterprise agreements
  • Infrastructure Australia board members step down ahead of review
  • Poorer young Australians suffer world-leading hit to housing: OECD
  • Foreigners slugged in first step towards Labor budget repair

  • Best of cartoons, July 21, 2022

  • Shorten intervenes to reverse gender-neutral language on Medicare form
  • Pay rises, not cupcakes: Childcare educators to strike nationally
  • Albanese ‘astonished’ by Morrison’s anti-government comments
  • ‘I’m pumped’: New senators assemble for an upper house boot camp
  • Indonesia pledges to halt foot and mouth outbreak as virus fragments found in Australia
  • Mask mandates are divisive but necessary to protect for all of us
  • In the Herald: July 22, 1987
  • Shiny hands and clean camels
  • Waste company fined more than $500,000 after worker killed by reversing skip loader
  • Perrottet concedes disappointment over trade trip being overshadowed by Barilaro controversy
  • ‘Guinea pig’ surfers test foot and mouth quarantine response
  • Michael Slater charged with breach of bail after police welfare check
  • Police release CCTV after 16-year-old stabbed in Sydney’s west during ‘fight over girl’
  • Joe Biden tests positive for COVID, experiencing mild symptoms
  • ‘Super Mario’ Draghi resigns after Italian government implodes
  • ‘Fear in the air’: Australian journalist freed by Taliban after forced denials
  • Secret Service and a gold-hued coffin: Ivana Trump’s funeral in style
  • US estimates 60,000 Russian casualties from Ukraine invasion
  • Scientists uncover the origin of mammal milestone: warm-bloodedness
  • Letter reveals China pressuring UN rights chief to bury Xinjiang report
  • Rishi is dishy but Truss is the true-blue choice for British PM
  • Taliban force Australian journalist to apologise for child sex slave reports
  • Donald Trump called officials ‘this week’ seeking overturn of 2020 election
  • COVID outbreak hits last ‘bigger nation’, two weeks before border due to open
  • Quidditch becomes quadball as sport distances itself from author JK Rowling
  • Teal Ryan just wild about Sally
  • Josh and the giant squid: Frydenberg heads to Goldman Sachs
  • Target Time and Superquiz, Friday, July 22
  • European Central Bank raises interest rates for first time in 11 years
  • ‘Cognitive dissonance’: Lean times loom for start-ups after bull market mania
  • Tesla profit falls but is stronger than expected
  • Rio tax avoidance settlement a ‘strong signal’ to other companies
  • ‘Unnecessarily alarmist’: Livestock leaders call for calm about foot and mouth disease
  • Woodside floats gas imports as ‘quick fix’ for Victorian energy crisis
  • Sydney to get a new rooftop bar as InterContinental hotel gets a $110m facelift
  • Aussie biotech’s prostate cancer imaging drug booms in the US
  • Are blockchain video games a fad, or the future?
  • The Wrap: Tech stocks win tug-of-war to push ASX higher
  • ‘Brings considerable value’: Josh Frydenberg lands job at Goldman Sachs
  • Australia (and the world) is still grappling with travel chaos. But it could have been worse
  • Zip Co shares surge as it vows to slow cash burn
  • Environment minister requests work pause on $4.5b Pilbara urea plant
  • Latex, frozen sperm and poo-eating beetles: The MCA gets its weird on
  • Domestic bliss becomes a living nightmare in this twisty thriller
  • ‘Nothing about us without us’: The idea that sparked Melbourne’s newest festival
  • The strange and compelling play that has no characters
  • The women of Hairspray on racism, sexism and having to hustle
  • The blood, tears and beheadings behind the ‘sickest band in the world’
  • Game of Thrones prequel fires up Comic-Con with first full trailer
  • Billy Porter’s romcom starts like a bad Clueless remake. How wrong I was
  • Like chardonnay: How the ‘new’ Opera House sounds
  • How to be a nepotism baby (it’s not as hard as the Hadids make out)
  • ‘What the hell just happened’: Fires force UK to face climate change future
  • Elevated flooding risk lingers over NSW for months to come
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: NY Times: 21 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Ukraine Tries to Make the Case That It Can Win, Citing Recent Strikes
  • Ukraine says it destroyed 6 Russian ammunition dumps and struck scores of other targets in the south.

entry.title: Guardian: 21 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Germany v Austria: Women’s Euro 2022 quarter-final – live!
  • England v South Africa: first women’s T20 international – live!
  • Grenfell fire inquiry ends with shocking reminder of the human cost
  • Sebastian Coe tips Jake Wightman to challenge for unprecedented treble
  • Tory leadership race: Rishi Sunak backs Rwanda asylum policy and calls himself ‘common sense’ Thatcherite – live
  • I Love That For You review – only one actor could pull off this shopping channel satire
  • Billie Eilish: Guitar Songs EP review – engulfed in the horror and hope of 2022

  • Drama averted as Kyler Murray agrees to $230.5m deal with Arizona through 2028

  • Irish people adopted abroad as children to get full access to their records
  • ‘Out of a horror movie’: Pennsylvania officer shoots snake around man’s neck

  • Outrage in Qatar over shooting of 29 dogs as it prepares for World Cup
  • Dungeons & Dragons and Superman: why Comic-Con is still the place to be

  • Barring media from hearing on Prince Philip’s will ‘did not harm public interest’, court hears

  • Vingegaard breaks Pogacar to win stage and all but seal Tour de France glory
  • Joe Biden tests positive for Covid and has ‘mild symptoms’, White House says

  • Boris Johnson said ‘hasta la vista, baby’ – but is he thinking ‘I’ll be back’?
  • ‘There are still battles to be had, but it’s incredible’: the female DJs finally getting their dues
  • Georgia Stanway is England’s wind-up toy with ‘a foot like a traction engine’
  • ‘You cannot imagine how crazy the system is’: Jafar Panahi’s film-maker son on Iranian censorship
  • Google under scrutiny over pledge to protect abortion location data

  • Leading figures urge drugs firm to lower price of ‘game-changing’ HIV prevention drug
  • Weeks of Truss and Sunak tearing lumps out of each other should bring nothing but joy for Labour | Polly Toynbee
  • Michelle Obama announces second book: ‘a toolbox to stay centred’
  • Destruction of nature as threatening as climate crisis, EU deputy warns
  • Déborah Lukumuena: ‘To be on screen with the body and skin I have is already very political’
  • Eddie Vedder cancels Pearl Jam concert due to throat damage from extreme weather
  • Spain bid farewell to Euro 2022 feeling angry, frustrated and robbed
  • Nancy Pelosi’s Taiwan trip ‘not a good idea right now’, says Biden
  • Facebook to restore chronological feed of posts from friends
  • Wild cheetahs to return to India for first time since 1952
  • England come from behind to storm into semis – Women’s Football Weekly
  • Sunak would not cut taxes before autumn 2023, in clear divide with Truss
  • European Central Bank raises interest rates for first time in 11 years
  • Zooey Deschanel: ‘Manic pixie dream girl? I’m not a girl. I’m a woman’
  • EU imposes sanctions on Russian motorcycle club and pro-Putin actors
  • Dining across the divide: ‘He supports a new Heathrow runway – we should invest in trains’
  • ‘It’s a feeling I’ve been chasing ever since’: Edinburgh festival debuts
  • Jamie T: The Theory of Whatever review | Alexis Petridis's album of the week
  • Volcano films – ranked!
  • Revealed: oil sector’s ‘staggering’ $3bn-a-day profits for last 50 years
  • Russia-Ukraine war at a glance: what we know on day 148 of the invasion
  • Kate’s choir, hidden portraits and J-Lo’s spouse – take the Thursday quiz
  • British drugs policy is punitive and contradictory. And now it’ll go backwards | Kojo Karam
  • A 17-minute flight? The super-rich who have ‘absolute disregard for the planet’
  • Italian PM Mario Draghi resigns after attempt to salvage coalition fails
  • This summer’s strikes are already working – unions, set your sights even higher | Polly Smythe
  • Suspected Fabergé egg found on Russian oligarch’s superyacht, US investigators say
  • Climate emergency is a legacy of colonialism, says Greenpeace UK
  • How a great English city sold itself to Abu Dhabi’s elite – and not even for a good price | Aditya Chakrabortty
  • Sri Lanka braced for more unrest as new president vows crackdown on ‘fascist’ protests
  • Caster Semenya out of world 5,000m as Coe signals tougher female sport rules
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: Financial Times: 21 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 21 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 21 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-21 18:45PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong protests: prosecutors press more serious charges against suspects for carrying zip ties to 2019 clashes
  • Fewer ferries lead to more Chinese white dolphin activity, Hong Kong group finds
  • Secondary schools in Hong Kong close ranks to appeal for class size reduction amid fall in pupil numbers
  • Hong Kong government ‘vehemently condemns’ US lawmakers’ call for sanctions on city’s prosecutors, says it shows disrespect for rule of law
  • Preventing heatstroke: Hong Kong government to include heat index in guidelines for employers to shield outdoor workers from high temperatures
  • Hong Kong leader John Lee tells government department to refund nearly HK$150,000 to district councillor who was charged for putting up 1,214 national flags on October 1
  • Hong Kong consumer price index rises by 1.5 per cent for past 6 months, overall inflation expected to ‘remain moderate in near term’
  • What does 1 degree on the thermostat mean for climate change?
  • Hong Kong police arrest 48 in crackdown on gambling syndicate controlled by triad
  • Hong Kong heatwave: residents in city’s subdivided flats, rooftop huts bear brunt of scorching weather
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong’s daily infections surpass 4,000, health minister to meet pandemic advisers on Covid strategy
  • Goodbye, An An: Hong Kong’s beloved giant panda euthanised after health declines from old age
  • End of story or new chapter? How Hong Kong’s independent booksellers are adapting to life under national security law
  • Sun Hung Kai offers steep discounts, deferred payment plans at Novo Land in Tuen Mun, yielding to first-home buyers’ demand for affordable housing
  • US lawmakers urge sanctions against Hong Kong prosecutors for ‘undermining’ city’s rule of law
  • Why China’s Gen Z see climate change as less important than their Western peers
  • Beijing warns US of ‘forceful’ reply to Taiwan visit by Pelosi
  • China smartphone brands such as Xiaomi continue to lead India market despite increased scrutiny, Canalys data shows
  • US scientists keep ties with Chinese peers despite crackdown: researcher
  • EU wants good ties with Beijing, but will still stand with Taiwan, top lawmaker says
  • Tencent to shut down Chinese NFT platform Huanhe a year after launch, report says, amid declining market
  • Chinese scientists unveil high-powered laser that can ‘write’ in the air
  • Two finalists in race for British PM unlikely to lead to big changes in Sino-UK relations
  • China’s banking crisis draws fresh scrutiny as economic slowdown highlights emerging risks
  • Chinese e-cigarette giant gets tobacco production licence, gaining foothold in country’s vaping market
  • China snaps up over US$2 billion of Russian LNG as energy trade bucks economic downturn
  • Baidu’s live-streamed annual tech conference briefly cut off on WeChat service amid China’s tightened online content rules
  • Taiwan: CIA chief warns Beijing looks determined to use force to take the island
  • China gaming crackdown: revenue, user base shrink for first time under harsh regulations, slowing economy
  • TikTok owner ByteDance spends record US$2.14 million on US lobbying amid scrutiny over app’s privacy and security practices
  • China to arrest former official of stockpiler Sinograin for corruption
  • Taiwan’s arms sales concerns will be conveyed, ex-US defence chief says
  • China deploys bad-loans expert to clean up Henan’s festering banking and property mess
  • Covid-19 in China: Shenzhen residents fear tighter controls on the way as authorities pledge to do all they can to stop new outbreak
  • China fines Didi Global US$1.2 billion, ends year-long probe
  • Tesla sells 75 per cent of bitcoin amid cryptocurrency crash, promises ‘record-breaking’ second-half results
  • China seen as better than EU in completing African projects, survey finds
  • China’s top Covid official denies authorities are easing controls and calls for swift response to new outbreaks
  • Joe Biden says call with Xi Jinping due within ‘next 10 days’, casts doubt on Pelosi trip to Taiwan
  • Thailand confirms first monkeypox infection on day WHO meets to decide global health emergency
  • India’s Sonia Gandhi questioned in historical money laundering case
  • Coronavirus: Tokyo hits pandemic record on rise of new Covid-19 subvariants
  • Will West Sumatra’s new law lead to more Islamic conservatism in Indonesia?
  • ‘Never again, never forget’, torture victim urges amid fears Philippines’ Marcos will repeat father’s human rights atrocities
  • ‘I want to leave Sri Lanka as soon as possible’: thousands jostle for passports to flee economic crisis
  • Can a hot-weather fee help protect Asian gig workers languishing in sweltering heat?
  • Coronavirus: Australia sees record deaths as Omicron variants push up hospital cases
  • Singapore’s hanging spate to continue as minister K. Shanmugam vows no let-up in tough laws to fight illegal drug use
  • Australia’s US$22 billion livestock industry under threat as foot and mouth disease found in imported meat
  • Distrust and unease loom as South Koreans grow wary of China’s clout
  • Italy to hold snap election after PM Mario Draghi quits
  • Joe Biden tests positive for Covid-19, ‘very mild symptoms’, White House says
  • BBC pays damages to former royal nanny over ‘false and malicious’ claims when she worked for Princess Diana
  • Vatican’s magnificent art belongs to us say Canada’s indigenous groups
  • Italy’s PM Mario Draghi resigns after coalition falls apart
  • Russia resumes critical gas supplies to Europe via Nord Stream
  • CIA director estimates 15,000 Russians killed in Ukraine war
  • Die in Ukraine or live in Russia: the poisoned choice for war refugees
  • UN reports numerous killings, rights abuses under Afghanistan’s Taliban
  • Donald Trump bids farewell to ex-wife Ivana at funeral in New York
  • Washington’s consensus on China rarely unites US lawmakers, research finds
  • Joe Biden unveils new climate funding, but stops short of declaring emergency
  • Italy PM Mario Draghi’s resignation likely as bid to save government implodes
  • China lobbying behind scenes at UN to block Xinjiang rights report’s publication, document shows
  • Britain blocks Chinese company’s deal to acquire camera technology as it could pose risk to national security
  • As China’s mortgage boycott revolt spreads, senior banking regulator assures steps to solve crisis
  • Baidu to put next-generation RT6 robocar into ride-hailing service in 2023, potentially slashing fares by half
  • Slowing growth shows China’s old playbook of property and infrastructure investment needs an update
  • Hong Kong stocks fall on concerns about China’s property crisis while Alibaba, other tech leaders trim losses as Didi probe ends
  • Hong Kong’s prime office market takes a breather as banks, other tenants pause after an eight-month leasing spree
  • Chinese headphone maker in Dongguan shuts down, intensifying country’s woes in manufacturing, cross-border e-commerce
  • Is your sustainable hotel really ‘net zero’? Almost certainly not – most forget to mention embodied carbon
  • Why you should drink water stored in a copper jug or pot – Ayurvedic experts explain its multiple health benefits, some backed up by science
  • Brazilian wax pros and cons: 5 reasons not to get rid of your pubic hair, and 5 dos and don’ts if you decide to get waxed
  • Instagram and TikTok influencers draw too many tourists, making destinations hard to book
  • The world’s most powerful passports in 2022: Japan top for fifth year in a row, as Asian countries dominate
  • Jelly cake enthusiast creates incredibly lifelike flower decorations and spreads the word in Hong Kong about the craft, popular in her native Malaysia and Singapore
  • Performance art festival in Hong Kong aims to expose a wider audience to a unique and powerful art form
  • Brad Pitt wore a skirt to premiere of Bullet Train film – it’s not the first time he’s challenged gender norms with fashion
  • In warm-up for an AI Celine Dion, AI choir and dancers accompany human orchestra in Hong Kong concert
  • Video game Stray, set in Hong Kong’s former Kowloon Walled City, in which you play a cat dodging danger, is a cyberpunk delight
  • Why you won’t see a Chinese villain in a Hollywood film any time soon
  • 20 celebrity kids who are BFFs: from Kim Kardashian and Jessica Simpson’s daughters, to J. Lo and Ben Affleck’s teens and Beyoncé’s Blue Ivy and Apple Martin, these stars’ mini-mes are bonding
  • What did Elvis Presley and Caitlyn Jenner share? Meet Linda Thompson, the King of Rock ‘n’ Roll’s girlfriend after Priscilla, who later married the Kardashians star – but was left out of the biopic
  • Meet Jean Arnault, Bernard Arnault’s youngest LVMH heir: the MIT graduate runs Louis Vuitton watches, did work experience at McLaren Racing, and was inspired by his Tag Heuer CEO brother Frédéric
  • Who’s the richest Wooga Squad member? Net worths, ranked: BTS’ V, Park Seo-joon, Park Hyung-sik, Choi Woo-shik and Peakboy are set to star in Disney+’s In the Soop, but who has the most millions?
  • Love it or hate it: why is 2000s fashion so controversial in K-pop? Blackpink’s Jennie and Girls’ Generation’s Yoona show off the style on Instagram, but not all millennials and Gen Zs are on board
  • Manolo Blahnik wins decades-long legal battle in China after trademark dispute: the shoemaker was made famous by Sex and the City, and joins Disney and Nike’s NBA star Michael Jordan in copyright victories
  • 5 times K-pop idols were swindled or betrayed by agency staff: BTS and Seventeen had their personal info leaked, Blackpink’s Lisa had money stolen, and Momoland’s Nancy was filmed undressing
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
